title: Colonial Experience
content:
آ آ آ آ آ آ آ آ  [By A New Chum]

آ آ آ آ آ آ آ آ  (Air: ï؟½So Early in the Morning.ï؟½)


آ آ آ آ When first I came to Sydney Cove
آ آ آ آ And up and down the streets did rove,
آ آ آ آ I thought such sights I neï؟½er did see
آ آ آ آ Since first I learnt my A, B, C.

آ آ آ آ آ آ آ آ آ آ آ آ آ آ آ آ آ آ آ آ آ آ آ آ آ آ آ آ  Chorus

آ آ آ آ آ آ آ آ  Oh! itï؟½s broiling in the morning,
آ آ آ آ آ آ آ آ  Itï؟½s toiling in the morning,
آ آ آ آ آ آ آ آ  Itï؟½s broiling in the morning,
آ آ آ آ آ آ آ آ  Itï؟½s toiling all day long.

آ آ آ آ Into the park I took a strollï؟½
آ آ آ آ I felt just like a buttered roll.
آ آ آ آ A pretty name ï؟½The Sunny South!ï؟½
آ آ آ آ A better one ï؟½The Land of Drouth!ï؟½

آ آ آ آ آ آ آ آ  Chorus: Oh! itï؟½s broiling, &c.

آ آ آ آ Next day into the bush I went,
آ آ آ آ On wild adventure I was bent,
آ آ آ آ Dame Natureï؟½s wonders Iï؟½d explore,
آ آ آ آ All thought of danger would ignore.

آ آ آ آ آ آ آ آ  Chorus: Oh! itï؟½s broiling, &c.

آ آ آ آ The mosquitoes and bull-dog ants
آ آ آ آ Assailed me even through my pants.
آ آ آ آ It nearly took my breath away
آ آ آ آ To hear the jackass laugh so gay!

آ آ آ آ آ آ آ آ  Chorus: Oh! itï؟½s broiling, &c.

آ آ آ آ This lovely country, Iï؟½ve been told,
آ آ آ آ Abounds in silver and in gold.
آ آ آ آ You may pick it up all day,
آ آ آ آ Just as leaves in autumn lay!

آ آ آ آ آ آ آ آ  Chorus: Oh! itï؟½s broiling, &c.

آ آ آ آ Marines will chance this yarn believe,
آ آ آ آ But bluejackets you canï؟½t deceive.
آ آ آ آ Such pretty stories will not fit,
آ آ آ آ Nor can I their truth admit.

آ آ آ آ آ آ آ آ  Chorus: Oh! itï؟½s broiling, &c.

آ آ آ آ Some say thereï؟½s lots of work to do.
آ آ آ آ Well, yes, but then, ï؟½twixt me and you,
آ آ آ آ A man may toil and broil all dayï؟½
آ آ آ آ The big, fat man gets all the pay,

آ آ آ آ آ آ آ آ  Chorus: Oh! itï؟½s broiling, &c.

آ آ آ آ Mayhap such good things there may be,
آ آ آ آ But you may have them all, for me,
آ آ آ آ Instead of roaming foreign parts
آ آ آ آ I wish Iï؟½d studied the Fine Arts!

آ آ آ آ آ آ آ آ  Chorus: Oh! itï؟½s broiling, &c.
meaning:
This evocative piece by Banjo Paterson (Andrew Barton) represents a masterful exploration of classic. The lines capture a profound emotional resonance, inviting the reader to reflect on the deeper themes of human experience and artistic expression.

In the broader context of Banjo Paterson (Andrew Barton)'s bibliography, these specific lines stand out for their clarity and poignancy. Whether you are searching for inspiration or a moment of quiet contemplation, this work offers a timeless perspective that transcends its original era.

At Linespedia, we have curated this specific poem to ensure it reaches a modern audience. We recommend sharing these lines as a digital poster or using them as a thoughtful caption to connect with others who appreciate the enduring beauty of verse.
